calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Apr-10-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. RICHARD ROTHSCHILD OF THE WESTERN CENTER, TEL. NO. 1-213-235-2624 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONERS;
ATTY. MICHAEL KEYS OF THE BAY AREA LEGAL AID, TEL. NO. 1-415-982-1300 ET. 6339 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONERS;
ATTY. JENNIFER C. ADDAMS, DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL, TEL. NO. 1-415-203-5382 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT.

RULING - A R G U E D; Petitioners Christina Litchenberger and Jane Redmond's motion for attorneys' fees is denied. Petitioners'' only ground for seeking fees is the catalyst theory per CCP 1021.5 for succeeding, by way of settlement of this case, in obtaining the elimination of the San Francisco Health Plan's "try and fail" requirement for covering unlisted drugs. Graham v. DaimerChrysler Corp. (2004) 34 Cal. 4th 553, 577 holds that "a plaintiff seeking attorney fees under a catalyst theory must first reasonably attempt to settle the matter short of litigation." Petitioners argue that: 1) their administrative appeals were sufficient prelitigation efforts to settle and, if not, 2) they are excused from making any prelitigation efforts to settle because any such efforts to settle would have been futile. Both arguments lack merit. Petitioners' first argument is similar to the argument rejected in Environmental Protection Information Center v. California Department of Forestry and Fire Protection (2010) 190 Cal. App. 4th 217, 236-37 that exhaustion of administrative remedies necessarily suffices to satisfy the prelitigation efforts to settle requirement. Nothing in the record on this motion shows that in either of petitioners' administrative hearings the issue of the legality of SFHP's try and fail requirement was dispositive or sufficiently highlighted that it can fairly be said that petitioners sought to settle their argument that the try and fail requirement was illegal. Significantly, the try and fail requirement was not cited as a basis for either of the two ALJ proposed decisions which were adopted by DHCS. Nor is there any evidence in the record on this motion that shows that a prelitigation effort by petitioners to resolve their disagreement with the try and fail requirement would have been futile. Indeed, there is considerable evidence to the contrary. Both sides agree that petitioners' first settlement proposal was made by letter dated July 24, 2014 by Mr. Keys and was responded to by letter dated September 3, 2014 by Ms. Addams. (Keys Dec. par. 23; Addams Dec. pars. 8-9). In her letter Ms. Addams agreed on behalf of DHCS to "require SFHP to amend" the try and fail requirement because that requirement is "not consistent" with 22 CCR 51313. This is precisely the relief obtained by petitioners for which they contend they are entitled to section 1021.5 fees, but nothing in the record on this motion suggests that, had petitioners made a prelitigation request to settle SFHP's try and fail requirement in the same or similar way that DHCS agreed to do in Ms. Addams' September 3, 2014 letter, DHCS would have refused. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Laura A. Reding, CSR 711 =302/hk
